subject: Mathematics
activityTitle: Fig. 1   Fig. 2 4. Arrange and paste these triangles on the cardboard as shown in Fig. 2 and
objective:
To draw a quadrilateral similar to a
given quadrilateral as per given scale
factor (less than 1).
Chart paper (coloured and white),
geometry   box,   cutter,   eraser,
drawing pins, glue, pins, sketch
pens, tape.
procedure:
1. Take a cut-out of the given quadrilateral ABCD from a coloured chart paper
and paste it on another chart paper [see Fig. 1].
2. Divide the base (here AB) of the quadrilateral ABCD internally in the ratio
(given by scale factor) at P [see Fig. 2].
3. With the help of ruler (scale),   join the diagonal AC of the quadrilateral
ABCD.
4. From P, draw a line-segment PQ||BC,   with the help of compasses
(set squares or paper folding) meeting AC at R [see Fig. 3].

This result can be used for similar figures other
than triangles also, which in turn helps in
preparing maps for plots etc.
